cross_scene_arcs.title: Commitment to Wally
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Candy's relationship with Wally evolves from pregnancy and abortion due to his deployment, through learning of his paralysis, to ultimately choosing him over Homer and reuniting as his caregiver.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Pregnant with Wally's baby, awaiting abortion
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Reunited with disabled Wally, sobbing and kissing him
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Adjusting to Wally's disability and their future together

cross_scene_arcs.title: Affair with Homer
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Candy bonds with Homer over shared loneliness, initiates intimacy, but their relationship is fraught with conflict due to her history with Wally, ending in a painful breakup.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Admires Homer after a medical procedure
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Crying uncontrollably after breaking up with Homer
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Emotional fallout from the ended affair

cross_scene_arcs.title: Conflict Over Abortion and Love
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Candy's decision to abort her pregnancy with Wally leads to post-abortion distress and fuels her romantic indecision between Wally and Homer.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Emotionally conflicted and arguing about her choice
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Guilt and fear related to the abortion and Wally's safety

cross_scene_arcs.title: Homer's Mentorship under Larch
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Homer is rescued and raised by Larch, becomes his medical assistant, conflicts over abortion practice, learns of Larch's protective deception, and eventually assumes his caretaker role.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Assumes Larch's duties as doctor and blesser for the orphans
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Ongoing moral conflict regarding abortion practice

cross_scene_arcs.title: Homer's Medical Development and Abortion Conflict
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Homer progresses from assisting births to confronting abortion, initially refusing but later performing one for Rose Rose, affirming his doctor identity while grappling with moral dilemmas.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Performs an abortion, asserting medical authority
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Personal stance on abortions remains unresolved

cross_scene_arcs.title: Homer's Departure and Romantic Entanglement
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Homer leaves St. Cloud's with Wally and Edna, then begins a physical relationship with Candy, marking his transition to life outside the orphanage.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: In a romantic relationship with Candy while away from St. Cloud's
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Relationship with Candy, who is Wally's girlfriend, creates ethical tension

cross_scene_arcs.title: Larch's Fatherly Bond and Mentorship of Homer
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Dr. Larch rescues Homer from abuse, mentors him in medicine, conflicts arise over ethical practices and Homer's independence, Larch defines Homer as his successor, and posthumously reveals protective deceptions.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Homer is rescued and in Larch's custody after abuse.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Homer learns of Larch's falsified X-ray and protective intent, reinforcing their bond.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: Whether Homer will fulfill Larch's vision as his successor.

cross_scene_arcs.title: Larch's Struggle to Preserve St. Cloud's Orphanage
cross_scene_arcs.arc_summary: Larch reflects on his unmet purpose at St. Cloud's, implements policies like destroying maternal records, confronts Board threats, forges credentials for Homer as successor, and dies.
cross_scene_arcs.start_state: Larch is at St. Cloud's with an unsolved problem he came to address.
cross_scene_arcs.end_state: Larch is dead, having engaged in deception to secure the orphanage's future.
cross_scene_arcs.unresolved_issue: The Board's intent to replace him and the success of his plans after death.
